what_it_does: Records a design realization on a noticeboard, listing which graph primitives are considered already implemented and identifying the unbuilt next step of wiring them into one walkable, scoped, observation-bearing graph.
observations: Noticeboard block item with YAML frontmatter (id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, timestamps, history).
Title frames a 'unifying realization' that has moved from assertion to grounded fact.

Enumerates six primitives said to exist: node/address resolver, mark/typed observation, relation/typed edge, address-frame scope cascade, injected self/relative-root identity, and a named/tracked but unbuilt walkable graph.
States the 'molecule' — integrating those atoms into one graph — is declared but not yet built.
Names the 'dragnet' as the natural first content to pour into the integrated graph.
Body is prose with a heading and bullet list, not code.
standouts: The pivot from an asserted pattern to a claim that each atomic primitive is confirmed to exist.
The explicit statement that the unified walkable graph is named and tracked but still unbuilt.
The recommendation that the dragnet be the first thing loaded into that integrated graph.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item schema with board:// address and a part_of link.
Uses markdown body with a top-level heading and bullet list.
No code symbols; it is a design/communication artifact rather than an implementation file.
concerns: The 'confirmed fact' claims are not evidenced inside this file.
The term 'dragnet' is used without being defined here.
The list mixes implementation status ('exists', 'NOT built') without citing artifacts.
notes: A channel-memory note asserting that the underlying graph primitives are proven and the next step is to build the unified walkable graph, starting with the dragnet.
questions: What exactly is the 'dragnet' in this context?
Where is the evidence that each listed primitive is confirmed to exist?
What is the 'most-specific-wins cascade' and how is it implemented?
What is the named, tracked, unbuilt graph artifact?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches the content — it records a grounded unifying realization — but the 'grounded' claim depends on evidence not shown in this file.
apparent_themes: Composition of proven primitives into an integrated model
intention_signals: Consolidate prior design discussion into a single realization, justify building a unified walkable graph by showing its parts already exist, and propose the dragnet as the first integration target.
novelty: Frames the whole system as one act — attaching a typed observation to an addressed node — and maps that act to a set of existing primitives, making the integrated graph the explicit remaining milestone.
contribution: A concise design thesis that maps existing system primitives to a single graph-integration goal and names the dragnet as the first content domain for that integration.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard design note asserting that the system's graph primitives (addressed nodes, typed marks, directed relations, scoped address frames, injected self-identity) are already implemented and the remaining step is to wire them into a single walkable, observation-bearing graph starting with the dragnet.
intention_for_embedding: To record and communicate the grounded realization that the next milestone is integrating existing graph primitives into a unified walkable graph and to propose the dragnet as its first population.
